transcript:
Agent: Thank you for calling SecureNet Insurance. This is Rachel. How can I help you today?

Customer: Rachel, I am absolutely furious right now. My claim was denied and nobody can tell me why. This is complete bullshit.

Agent: I am really sorry to hear that Calvin. A denied claim with no explanation is completely unacceptable and I want to get to the bottom of this right now. Can I pull up your account?

Customer: Yes. My policy number is PN-8812940 and my SSN is 567-43-8812.

Agent: Thank you. And your date of birth for verification?

Customer: 09/04/1975. And I am telling you if this is not resolved today I am filing a complaint with the state insurance commissioner. I have had it with this company.

Agent: That is absolutely your right and I do not blame you. Let me look at the claim. I can see claim number CL-2290445 filed on April 28th for a home repair following storm damage. The denial reason listed is quote insufficient documentation.

Customer: Insufficient documentation? I sent 47 photos, two contractor estimates, and a damn weather report. What else do they want?

Agent: I hear you, and looking at what was submitted that documentation looks thorough to me. I am escalating this to our claims review team right now as an urgent case. I am also noting that this denial appears to have been made in error.

Customer: It had better be an error. I have been paying premiums for eleven years without a single claim. This is how you treat loyal customers? It is absolutely ridiculous.

Agent: You are right and your loyalty absolutely matters. I am making sure the review team gets this today, not in the normal 5 day queue. Can I confirm your email and best contact number so the claims manager can reach you directly?

Customer: Email is calvin.briggs@myemail.com and my cell is 404-882-5531.

Agent: Got it. I have flagged the file and the claims manager will call you at 404-882-5531 within 24 hours. I am genuinely sorry for how this has been handled.

Customer: I appreciate you taking it seriously at least. More than I can say for whoever denied it.

Agent: I understand. I will make sure this gets the attention it deserves. Is there anything else I can help with?

Customer: No. Just get this sorted.

Agent: We will. Thank you Calvin.
